common.noEntries: No entries found in the enabled compendiums.
common.selectHint: Choose an option from the list to see its full description.
common.noResultsFilters: No matches — try adjusting the filters.

cancel.title: Discard unsaved choices?
cancel.body: Nothing you've picked has been saved yet. If you close now, those choices will be discarded — you'll have to make them again.

quickBuild.hint: Fill everything with the recommended build for this class — a suggested background, a random species, skills, and spells. You can adjust any step afterwards.
quickBuild.confirmBody: Quick Build replaces your background, species, name, ability scores, spells, and other choices with the recommended set. Continue?
quickBuild.partial: Quick Build finished with some gaps — check the remaining steps.
quickBuild.failed: Quick Build failed; your choices were left as they were.

cancel.title: Discard this level-up?
cancel.body: Nothing has been applied to your character yet. If you close now, everything you've chosen here — hit points, features, spells, and other picks — will be discarded.

notify.applyFailed: Level-up could not be applied. See the console for details.
notify.spellsFailed: The chosen spells could not be added. The level-up itself was applied — add them from the sheet.
notify.takeoverFailed: The level-up could not be prepared. The character was not changed — try again or use the system's own level-up.
notify.choicesUnsupported: This level needs choices the simple level-up doesn't handle yet. Use the system's advancement flow.

class.label: Class
class.hint: Pick which class gains this level.
class.switchBody: Switching class discards everything you've chosen for this level-up so far — hit points, features, spells, and other picks.

add.hint: Start a new class at level 1 instead of advancing one you already have.
add.off: Multiclassing is disabled in this world.
add.none: There are no other classes available to take.
add.browseHint: Pick the class to begin at level 1. Locked classes list the ability score you are missing.

hp.label: Hit Points
hp.intro: Increase your {class}'s hit points — take the reliable average, or roll the hit die and take your chances.

choices.label: Features
choices.replaceHint: Your current choice is ticked. Untick it to swap, then pick another.

asi.label: Ability Scores
asi.hint: Raise your ability scores by {points} points, up to +{cap} in any one.
asi.improvementDesc:
You gain the following benefits:
{points} ability score increases
asi.locked: This choice can't raise this ability

subclass.label: Subclass
subclass.intro: Your class branches here — choose the subclass that shapes the rest of your journey.

grant.label: Spell
grant.prompt: Choose the spellcasting ability for this spell.

spells.label: Spells
spells.intro: Your {class} training opens new magic — choose the spells you learn at this level.
spells.noneNeeded: No new spells to choose this level.
spells.swapHint: You may also swap one spell you know — pick a known spell and choose Swap Out to trade it for another.
spells.selectHint: Pick a spell from the list to read it, then learn it.

notify.noPermission: You do not have permission to create actors.
notify.indexFailed: Could not read the compendiums. Check that the dnd5e content packs are enabled.
notify.buildFailed: Character creation failed. See the console for details.

details.label: Details
details.hint: Enter a name for your character.
details.subtitle: Give your character a name and, if you like, the finer points of who they are.
details.generateName: Roll a random name for this species
details.optional: Optional — flesh out who they are.

placeholder.bonds: Who or what matters to them?

spells.label: Spells
spells.intro: Choose the cantrips and 1st-level spells your {class} knows at level 1.
spells.selectedNone: None chosen yet — pick from the list below.
spells.noCaster: This class has no spellcasting at level 1 — nothing to choose here.
spells.empty: No spells found for this class in the enabled compendiums.
spells.selectHint: Pick a spell from the list to read it and add it to your character.

featSpells.label: Feat Spells
featSpells.intro: {feat} grants spells — choose a spell list, its casting ability, and the spells you learn.
featSpells.abilityHint: Sets the attack bonus and save DC for these feat spells. Pick the mental ability your character is strongest in.
featSpells.alreadyKnown: You already know this spell from another source, so it can't be chosen here.
featSpells.pickListFirst: Choose a spell list above to see the spells you can learn.
featSpells.hint: Choose {count} more feat spell(s) to continue.
featSpells.none: None of your choices grant extra spells — nothing to choose here.

choices.label: Choices
choices.none: Your chosen class, background, and species need no further decisions.

equipment.label: Equipment
equipment.hint: Choose the starting gear granted by your class and background.
equipment.none: Your chosen class and background grant no starting-equipment choices.

abilities.label: Ability Scores
abilities.rollPrompt: Roll to generate six scores, then assign them to your abilities.
abilities.hintAssign: Assign a score to every ability.
abilities.hintRoll: Roll your ability scores, then assign them.

bgAbilities.label: Ability Increase
bgAbilities.hint: Raise your ability scores by {points} points, up to +{cap} in any one.
bgAbilities.none: This background grants no ability increase.

choice.emptyNoteSkills: Pick skill proficiencies first to choose Expertise.

blurb.mastery: Weapon mastery lets you use a weapon's special mastery property when you attack with it.
blurb.weapon: Weapon proficiency adds your proficiency bonus to attack rolls made with these weapons.
blurb.skills: Skill proficiency adds your proficiency bonus to ability checks made with that skill.
blurb.tool: Tool proficiency adds your proficiency bonus when you work with these tools.
blurb.languages: Languages let you speak, read, and write with those who know them.
blurb.armor: Armor proficiency lets you wear this armor without penalty.
blurb.saves: Saving-throw proficiency adds your proficiency bonus to that saving throw.
blurb.resist: These reduce or ward off certain kinds of harm.
blurb.expertise: Expertise doubles your proficiency bonus for the chosen skills.
blurb.size: Your species can be more than one size — pick the token size your character uses.
blurb.spellAbility: Pick which ability score you use to cast this spell.
blurb.itemChoice: Pick from the options below to add to your character.

launchButton.name: Show launch button
launchButton.hint: Adds a “Simple Character Builder” button to the Actors sidebar for anyone allowed to create actors.

contextMenu.name: Show Level Up in right-click menu
contextMenu.hint: Adds a “Level Up” option to a character's right-click menu in the Actors sidebar, opening the same level-up flow as the sheet button.

pointBuyBudget.name: Point-buy budget
pointBuyBudget.hint: Number of points available when using the Point Buy method (standard rules use 27).

rollFormula.name: Ability roll formula
rollFormula.hint: Dice formula rolled for each ability when using the Roll method (standard rules use 4d6kh3).

displayMode.name: Display mode
displayMode.hint: How the Character Creator opens: Fullscreen covers the whole screen; Windowed opens a draggable, resizable window.

mode.name: Module mode
mode.hint: Which experiences the module owns. Creation + Level-Up (the default) does both; Creation only leaves levelling to the system; Level-Up only hides the creator and keeps just the calmer, one-screen-per-level level-up flow.
mode.hintEmber: The Ember module is active and provides its own character creation, so this module runs in Level-Up only mode: the creator is hidden and only the level-up experience is taken over.

levelUpButton.name: Show Level Up button
levelUpButton.hint: Adds a “Level Up” button to the character sheet's header for the character's owner. Levelling through the sheet's own class controls still works either way.

allowMulticlass.name: Allow multiclassing
allowMulticlass.hint: Lets players add a new class when levelling up. With prerequisites shows only classes whose ability requirements (13+ in the primary ability of both the current and the new class) are met; Without prerequisites offers every class.

levelUpHpMode.name: Level-up hit points
levelUpHpMode.hint: Which hit-point options players see when levelling up. Player's choice also offers taking the maximum or typing a value; Average or roll matches the written rules; Average only applies the average automatically.
levelUpHpMode.choice: Player's choice (average, max, roll, or manual)

levelUpHpRollToChat.name: Post hit-die rolls to chat
levelUpHpRollToChat.hint: When a player rolls hit points during a level-up, also post the roll to chat so the whole table can see the result.
